title: Smoky Slovakian Pork Stew
description: A hearty, comforting Slovakian-inspired stew made with tender pork, smoky flavors, and comforting vegetables.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the pork cubes on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the browned pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the chopped carrot and potato, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the browned pork back to the pot, along with the light beer, soup, black pepper, and Slovakian paprika. Stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the pork is tender, about 2 hours.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or dill, if desired.
